metrics.label: MOU Dead — War Formally Restarted
metrics.sub: Trump: MOU was 'only a test.' Iran deputy FM: 'no obligations.' Trump War Powers notification to Congress Jul 10 — 60-day clock started. Blockade reimposed 20:00 GMT Jul 14. CENTCOM struck Iran 4 consecutive nights. 180 Iran MPs demanding permanent Hormuz codification and end to all negotiations. No diplomatic framework, no interlocutor, no channel. Both sides confirm the war has formally restarted.

metrics.label: Dual Chokepoint Threat
metrics.sub: Houthi declared full air and naval blockade. Saudi airports struck (Abha). Bab el-Mandeb closure explicitly threatened by Houthi officials — 'will join with Hormuz.' Saudi East-West Pipeline bypass to Yanbu neutralised if both straits close. ~25% of global oil/gas supply blocked. No modern precedent for simultaneous dual-chokepoint closure. EASA warned airlines against all Gulf state airspace.

metrics.label: CPI Ceasefire Mirage
metrics.sub: June CPI beat across the board: headline -0.4% MoM (vs -0.1% exp), 3.5% YoY (vs 3.8% exp). Core flat at 2.6% YoY. Energy -5.7%. Biggest monthly drop since April 2020. BUT reflects ceasefire window — Brent at $68 in late June. July data will reverse with Brent at $85+ and blockade. Warsh: 'Mission accomplished is not my view.' De Haan: $4 gas in 7-10 days, $5 diesel by Friday.

metrics.label: Ras Laffan at 45% Capacity
metrics.sub: Satellite thermal: only 34.7 mtpa vs 77 mtpa nameplate. RasGas Trains 4 and 6 multi-year write-offs from March strikes. Zero LNG carriers through Hormuz since Jul 11. Direct threat to EU 90% storage mandate (currently 51.84%). EU gas storage: Netherlands 29.86%, Germany 43.94% — all below 2017-2021 average.

metrics.label: SPR at 316.5mb — 64mb Usable
metrics.sub: Three thresholds: 714mb authorised, 252.4mb EPCA legal floor, ~150mb structural minimum. Usable buffer: ~64mb. At 6mb/wk draw rate: ~10 weeks to legal floor (September-October). Converges with Iran's FX window, EU storage mandate, and midterm pressure peak. Bank earnings record (JPM equities +86%) but Dimon: 'as good as it gets.' 30Y at 5.09%, 10Y at 4.58%.

metrics.label: First Oil Infrastructure Strike
metrics.sub: First confirmed US strike on oil infrastructure at Kharg Island — western jetty pumping station and pipelines. Every previous strike deliberately spared oil infrastructure. Kish Island water/power infrastructure also struck. HIMARS/ATACMS fired from Bahrain into Iran — first surface-to-surface from host nation. Iran retaliating with cluster munitions against Bahrain (first time — previously only against Israel). UAE tankers struck by Iranian cruise missiles in Omani waters (1 killed). Kuwait intercepted 39 projectiles. Chinese tanker Rich Starry defied blockade — first direct challenge.

scenarios.name: Resolved by Mid-2026

horizons.current: MOU formally dead — both sides. Trump: 'only a test.' Iran deputy FM Gharibabadi: 'no obligations.' 180 Iran MPs demanding permanent Hormuz codification, end to all negotiations. IAEA access flatly refused. France conditioning sanctions on full nuclear rollback. No interlocutor, no framework, no channel.
horizons.near: Sc1 requires entirely new diplomatic architecture from zero. Iran Parliament legislation would institutionally lock out future negotiations. Pezeshkian politically weakened (ISW: scapegoated for MOU failure). No figure has both IRGC backing and Washington credibility.
horizons.extended: 12–18 months minimum from zero to framework. Earliest realistic Sc1 in H2 2027. Physical constraints unchanged: mine-clearing 6–8 months, Tomahawk replenishment to 2030, THAAD/Patriot to 2029.

scenarios.trigger: Requires regime-level shift — new Iranian interlocutor with IRGC mandate plus US willingness to restart from zero. Currently impossible.
scenarios.toMove: Watch: Any Supreme Leader-level signal; IAEA channel restoration; post-midterm US posture shift.

scenarios.name: Managed Stalemate

horizons.current: Functionally extinct. War Powers notification formalizes belligerency. Blockade reimposed. Houthi full Bab el-Mandeb blockade declared. Both sides confirm MOU dead. ISW: Pezeshkian implored Mojtaba to accept MOU — he was the only stalemate-enabling actor and is now politically broken.
horizons.near: No stalemate-enabling mechanism exists. Every mediator struck. Iran Parliament moving to codify Hormuz control legislatively. Gulf states transitioning from mediators to co-belligerents (UAE tankers struck, Bahrain as ATACMS platform).
horizons.extended: Sc2 only revives with a new Iranian leadership configuration and a US administration willing to restart from scratch. Neither condition visible on any timeline.

scenarios.trigger: New ceasefire framework negotiated under active combat. Requires third-party broker with credibility on both sides. None visible after mediator strikes.
scenarios.toMove: Watch: China mediation signal; Araghchi-Oman outcome; mutual exhaustion indicators; any IRGC-backed diplomatic channel.

scenarios.name: Prolonged — Re-Escalation Risk

horizons.current: DOMINANT. Multi-front kinetic exchange: CENTCOM 4 consecutive strike nights, blockade reimposed Jul 14. Iran retaliating against Gulf states, tankers, US bases. Houthi declaring Bab el-Mandeb closure — dual chokepoint scenario with no modern precedent. BUT oil flowing at ~79% via dark/escorted routes (LVision). Every's 'Comfortably Bomb' thesis: market anaesthesia from SPR + low Chinese imports + workarounds. Window is finite — weeks to months.
horizons.near: Sc3 conditions actively maturing toward Sc4. September-October convergence: SPR hits legal floor (~64mb usable at 6mb/wk), Iran's FX window closes (60-90 days post-blockade), midterm pressure peaks. $4 gas / $5 diesel (De Haan). Crack spreads at $61/bbl (Kemp) — near-record. Ukraine refinery campaign adds second front to refined products crisis.
horizons.extended: Infrastructure half-life thesis accelerating. Kharg oil infrastructure struck for first time. Ras Laffan at 45% capacity. Every month of bypass construction reduces Iran's future Hormuz leverage. Convergence toward Sc4 boundary tightening.

scenarios.trigger: Sustained escalation cycle with no off-ramp mechanism. US Saturday deadline, Israeli operational timeline, and Iran closure enforcement are near-term triggers.
scenarios.toMove: Watch: Iran response to Saturday deadline; Strait closure enforcement pattern; Asian market repricing; yen carry trade stability; Syria-Iraq pipeline deal.

scenarios.name: Regime Change / Collapse

horizons.current: New briefing high at 33%. Pickaxe Mountain nuclear facility publicly on target list. First US strike on Kharg oil infrastructure (western jetty). HIMARS/ATACMS fired from Bahrain into Iran. ISW: Pezeshkian scapegoated, anti-negotiations camp consolidating. Insider source (partially validated) predicts boots on the ground.
horizons.near: Sc3-to-Sc4 boundary compressing. Iran Parliament codifying Hormuz control = institutional rigidity that makes compromise structurally impossible. Economic asphyxiation timeline: 60-90 day FX window + blockade. Joint IRGC/Army operations cut against defection thesis — but sustained infrastructure destruction compounds.
horizons.extended: If US wins Battle of Hormuz, Iran becomes 'regionally shrunken, economically caged, geopolitically ignored' (Every/Rabobank). Sc4 arrives as acceleration from Sc3, not a separate event. Post-regime planning remains absent.

scenarios.trigger: Institutional lock-in now active: legislative codification, doctrinal commitment, operational enforcement. Cumulative pressure from Sc3 conditions compounding rather than single trigger.
scenarios.toMove: Watch: Mojtaba Jul 14 memorial; IRGC factional dynamics; Israeli operational signals; Gulf state military coordination; nuclear doctrine shift.

filters.label: Multi-Front Kinetic Exchange
filters.impact: Sc1 -1pp, Sc3 +4pp. CENTCOM 4 consecutive strike nights. Blockade reimposed Jul 14. Iran retaliating against Gulf states, tankers, US bases. HIMARS/ATACMS from Bahrain. Kharg oil infrastructure struck. 300+ targets since Jul 7. War Powers 60-day clock started.

filters.label: IRGC / Civilian Split — Pezeshkian Scapegoated
filters.impact: Sc1 -2pp, Sc3 +5pp. ISW/NYT: Pezeshkian implored Mojtaba to accept MOU — only stalemate-enabling actor now politically broken. Anti-negotiations camp consolidating. Iran Parliament 180 MPs demanding end to all negotiations. Institutional lock-in against future diplomacy.

filters.label: Netanyahu Re-Entry Signal
filters.impact: Sc1 -1pp, Sc3 +3pp. Netanyahu: "Don't count on silence." Israel hinting at re-entering conflict. Lebanon Rome talks ongoing but Hezbollah rejecting framework. Oct 27 election remains structural derailer.

filters.label: Bond/Yield Stress — Stagflation Signal
filters.impact: Sc2 -3pp. 30Y at 5.09%. 10Y at 4.58%. Yield curve steepening = stagflation pricing. CPI headline relief (-0.4%) but July data will reverse. Warsh: no forward guidance. Fed split 50/50 on hikes.

filters.label: EU Gas + Qatar LNG Crisis
filters.impact: Sc2 -2pp, Sc3 +3pp. EU storage 51.84%, must reach 90% by October. Ras Laffan at 45% capacity. Zero LNG through Hormuz since Jul 11. Houthi Bab el-Mandeb threat complicates Red Sea alternative.

filters.label: US Munitions Depleted + SPR Draining
filters.impact: Sc2 +2pp. Tomahawks to 2030, THAAD/Patriots to 2029. Zero THAAD deliveries 2026. SPR at 316.5mb — ~64mb above legal floor. ~10 weeks to EPCA threshold. Congress appropriated zero replacement funding.

filters.label: Refined Products Crisis ($61 Crack)
filters.impact: Sc1 -1pp, Sc3 +3pp. Kemp: 3-2-1 crack at $61/bbl (near-record). De Haan: $4 gas / $5 diesel. Ukraine hit all 11 of Russia's top refineries. Russia diesel ban. Dual-front war of the refineries.

filters.label: MOU Dead — No Diplomatic Framework
filters.impact: Sc1 -2pp, Sc3 +2pp. Both sides confirm. Trump: "only a test." Iran: "no obligations." Every/Rabobank: collapsed over easiest contradiction (tolls) — harder issues even more intractable.

filters.label: Dual Chokepoint Threat
filters.impact: Sc1 -1pp, Sc2 -1pp, Sc3 +2pp. Houthi full blockade declared. Bab el-Mandeb threatened alongside Hormuz. Saudi Yanbu bypass neutralised. ~25% global supply at risk. No modern precedent.

filters.label: Iran 80mb Pre-Blockade Export + FX Window
filters.impact: Sc2 -1pp, Sc3 +2pp, Sc4 -1pp. TankerTrackers: Iran shipped 80mb ($6B) in 26 days before blockade. 60mb floating storage inside blockade line. FX revenue window 60-90 days. Fiscal crunch Sept-Oct.

countries.name: USA
countries.energy: Net crude exporter (12.9mb/d). Domestic refining largely insulates from diesel shock.
countries.vulnerability: IRGC declared Hormuz closed. 300+ targets struck. Iran retaliating across 6 countries. Brent ~$82. 10Y yield 4.59%. 30Y at 5.07%. Diesel crack spread $72.90/bbl. SPR at ~313mb drawing 6.2mb/wk while exporting record 8.7mb/d refined products. Gasoline $3.80/gal, diesel $4.80/gal. USS Ford in maintenance.
countries.detail: Active combat intensifying: 300+ targets struck across 3 nights plus continued Jul 11-12. Iran retaliating with missiles at US bases in 6 countries. No US casualties yet — the threshold Trump set for full escalation. SPR at ~313mb and falling 6.2mb/wk while the US simultaneously exports record 8.7mb/d refined products into global shortage. Diesel crack spread hit record $72.90/bbl (3.6× seasonal norm). Gasoline at $3.80/gal, diesel at $4.80/gal. Commodity Context analysis: ‘too much crude, not enough refined products’ — global refinery capacity cannot keep up with demand, and the crude-to-products divergence is structural, not cyclical. US RINs doubled in 2026, adding further domestic fuel cost pressure. 30Y yield at 5.07% (bond vigilante threshold). Fed rate hike cycle now expected. Japan-Treasury feedback loop active. USS Ford entered Norfolk for PIA Jul 7. Midterms November 3.

countries.name: Canada
countries.energy: Net exporter — LNG and crude beneficiary. Significant domestic refining capacity.
countries.vulnerability: CPI 2.8% in April. Gasoline +28.6% YoY. Technical recession: Q1 GDP -0.1%. Fuel excise suspended. LNG Canada Phase 1 ramping — strategic value rising as Qatar capacity structurally impaired.
countries.detail: The MOU collapse and military re-escalation compound Canada’s existing stagflationary squeeze. Technical recession was confirmed with Q1 GDP at -0.1%, driven primarily by tariffs. Oil sands benefit from higher crude with WTI at $78+ well above breakeven, but this cannot offset the broader tariff-driven investment drought. LNG Canada Phase 1 at Kitimat is ramping and provides strategic supply value that rises as Strait closure threatens Qatar’s remaining exports. Canada’s potash reserves provide partial fertiliser insulation. The Alberta oil windfall versus broader national contraction deepens a regional divergence that complicates federal fiscal and monetary policy. US-Canada bridge toll deal (Reuters Jul 11) offers minor positive signal on bilateral relations.

countries.name: UK
countries.energy: High import dependence — LNG, piped gas, diesel and jet fuel
countries.vulnerability: Russia diesel ban + Ukraine shadow fleet destruction compounds Hormuz refined products exposure. Diesel crack at record $72.90/bbl. UK imports 2.5× domestic diesel production. BoE projects rising inflation through Q4. Gilt yields above 5%. Dual supply shock: Iran crude via Hormuz + Russia refined products via drone campaign.
countries.detail: The UK is the most exposed G7 economy to the refined products channel, importing 2.5× its domestic diesel production and 3.1× jet fuel. The dual supply shock is now fully operational: Iran constraining crude and products via Hormuz while Ukraine’s drone campaign has destroyed ~90 Russian shadow fleet vessels in one week and halted Don-Azov Canal shipping. Russia’s diesel export ban directly hits the UK supply chain. Diesel crack spread at record $72.90/bbl. The Ofgem price cap moderation was a one-time fiscal buffer that does not repeat. Gilt yields remain above 5%. PM Burnham locked defence at 2.7% GDP by 2030 (vs Germany 3.7%, Poland 4.5%). If full Strait closure materializes, Asian refinery-to-UK finished diesel delivery takes weeks, creating an acute physical supply gap.

countries.name: Germany
countries.energy: Critically exposed — LNG, industrial gas, and diesel dependent
countries.vulnerability: Russia diesel ban + shadow fleet destruction compounds existing energy transmission. Diesel crack at record $72.90/bbl. EU gas storage behind schedule for Oct 90% target. DAX fell 2.54% on ceasefire collapse. VW axing half its car models. Budget deficit 4.2% breaching Stability Pact.
countries.detail: Germany’s industrial exposure operates on three vectors simultaneously: gas, electricity, and diesel. The dual supply shock from Iran (Hormuz) and Russia (drone campaign + diesel ban) is now fully compounding. EU gas storage must reach 90% by October and is behind schedule. Diesel crack spread at record $72.90/bbl directly pressures German logistics and heavy industry. Volkswagen axing half its car models (Telegraph Jul 10) signals deepening industrial contraction. Budget deficit at 4.2% breaches the EU Stability Pact 3% threshold. Rearmament commitment targeting 3.7% GDP creates an impossible fiscal trinity alongside energy subsidies and rising Bund yields. FT warns to ‘prepare for a perilous summer in markets.’

countries.name: S. Africa
countries.primaryTheme: Food Security + Financial Stress + Fuel Security
countries.energy: Imports virtually all crude oil and refined products. No domestic crude production.
countries.vulnerability: Russia diesel ban compounds existing shock. Diesel crack at record $72.90/bbl. Diesel +35.4% m/m in April (record since 2008). CPI jumped to 4.0%. SARB raised repo 25bp to 7.00%. R17.2B diesel subsidy. Dual supply shock: Iran + Russia simultaneously constrained.
countries.detail: South Africa carries the most violent fuel shock of any country in the briefing. April data showed the fuel index surging 18.2% month-on-month — the steepest move since the current CPI series began in 2008. The MOU collapse and IRGC Hormuz closure declaration risk a second acute shock. SARB raised the repo rate to 7.00%. The three-front inflation crisis is fully active: fuel shock, services/transport pass-through, and food pressures from El Niño lagging 6-12 months. South Africa imports virtually all crude and refined products with no domestic production buffer. The R17.2 billion diesel levy relief dwarfs the R10 billion contingency reserve.

countries.name: Australia
countries.primaryScenario: Sc1 most positive / Sc3 high risk
countries.energy: Major LNG exporter (+140% spot) but 90%+ refined fuel import dependent
countries.vulnerability: Russia diesel ban adds second supply shock to 33-day diesel reserves. Diesel crack at record $72.90/bbl. 90%+ fuel import dependent with only 2 domestic refineries. Dual supply shock: Iran Hormuz + Russia refined products. Australian editorial: ‘politicians aren’t up to lead in war.’
countries.detail: Australia is 90%+ dependent on imported liquid fuel with only 33 days of diesel reserves — well below the IEA 90-day standard. The diesel crack spread at record $72.90/bbl directly quantifies Australia’s exposure. The dual supply shock (Hormuz + Russia diesel ban + Ukraine shadow fleet destruction) compounds existing vulnerability. RBA hiked to 4.35% and trimmed mean inflation at 3.4% continues climbing. Treasury’s prolonged conflict scenario is now the operating reality, not a tail risk. If full Strait closure materializes, the 33-day diesel buffer becomes the binding constraint on the entire economy. Physical Hormuz reopening requires 6+ months of mine-clearing.

countries.name: Colombia
countries.primaryScenario: Sc3 high impact — new president inherits bind
countries.primaryTheme: Food Security + Financial Stress + Transition Watch
countries.energy: Net oil exporter but domestic fuel import dependent for refined products
countries.vulnerability: CPI 5.8% in May. Policy rates at 11.25%. Food 6% YoY. New president inherits severe monetary policy bind. Diesel-dependent transport and fertiliser exposure.
countries.detail: Colombia entered the crisis with structural inflation already above 5%, and the Iran escalation layers on top of existing domestic demand pressures. CPI at 5.8% with policy rates at 11.25% creates the most severe monetary policy bind of any country in the briefing. The new president inherits this bind with no easy policy options. Colombia is a net oil exporter but depends on refined product imports for transport diesel and fertiliser — the compound exposure channel. Food inflation runs at 6% year-on-year with El Niño threatening Arabica coffee production and fertiliser shortages threatening broader agricultural output.

countries.name: Ukraine
countries.energy: War-damaged supply chains — compounded by Hormuz refined product and crude disruption
countries.vulnerability: Shadow fleet campaign success: ~90 vessels struck in one week, Don-Azov Canal halted, Syzran/Ilsky/Omsk refineries hit. Russian refining one-third offline. Trump granted licence to produce Patriot missiles. But Patriot interceptor stocks exhausted (Zelensky Jul 11). Russian territorial advances slowed by >50% in H1 2026 (Syrskyi).
countries.detail: Ukraine’s asymmetric campaign against Russian energy infrastructure achieved breakthrough scale in Jul 7-12: ~90 shadow fleet vessels struck in one week at ‘industrial scale,’ Don-Azov Canal shipping halted, Kerch Strait transit frozen, Syzran refinery (Samara) struck Jul 12, Ilsky and Omsk hit earlier. Russian refining one-third offline. Gazprom shares at 17-year low. This success directly compounds the Iran-Hormuz supply shock — the dual supply squeeze on global diesel is a product of both wars simultaneously. However, Zelensky warned Jul 11 that Patriot interceptor stocks are exhausted, and Ukraine faces growing air defence crisis. Russian territorial advances slowed by more than half in H1 2026 (Syrskyi). The OFAC revocation of Iran’s export license paradoxically benefits Russia by tightening global supply and supporting Russian export prices.

riskFactors.name: IRGC Geographic Escalation — Active Full-Spectrum
riskFactors.rationale: UPDATED A21: CENTCOM 4 consecutive strike nights. Blockade reimposed Jul 14. First oil infrastructure strike (Kharg western jetty). HIMARS/ATACMS fired from Bahrain. Iran retaliating with cluster munitions against Bahrain — first use outside Israel, maximises area damage. 3 more tankers attacked Jul 13 (JMIC: threat level 'severe'). Iran struck Kuwait (39 projectiles), Bahrain, Jordan. IRGC deploying 3,000 special forces to Iran-Iraq border. Only 5 vessels transiting Hormuz in 24h — all Chinese/Iranian. Chinese tanker Rich Starry defied blockade directly. Score: L5 × I5 = 25 (Critical max).

riskFactors.name: IRGC / Civilian Government Split (‘Juntocracy’)
riskFactors.rationale: UPDATED A20: Iran’s ‘errant faction’ gambit revealed. Iranian negotiators privately told US officials that a hardline faction undermined talks by attacking shipping. ISW assesses this is a tactical play — the IRGC chain of command authorized the attacks, not a rogue element. The gambit lets the regime sustain talks (Araghchi in Oman Jul 11) and attempt to reap economic benefits while the IRGC continues to enforce Strait control. Press TV denied any ‘rogue forces’ operating. The split is one of immediate tactical means (negotiations vs military action), not strategic ends — both factions agree Hormuz control is essential leverage. Score: L5 × I5 = 25.

riskFactors.name: Israel-US Gap / Netanyahu Undermining — Principal-Agent Trap
riskFactors.rationale: Lebanon Rome talks Jul 15-16 confirmed. Lebanon participating despite earlier conditions re pilot zone withdrawal. Hezbollah Secretary General Qassem rejected framework, demanded IDF complete withdrawal. LAF refuses to initiate ‘internal confrontation’ with Hezbollah over civil war risk. IDF preparing for long stay in southern Lebanon as Hezbollah regroups. Netanyahu’s Oct 27 election remains structural derailer. Score: L5 × I5 = 25.

riskFactors.name: Trump-Netanyahu Principal-Agent Trap — CEASEFIRE FRAUD CONFIRMED
riskFactors.rationale: Confirmed and continuing. Israel’s Jun 26 Trilateral Agreement Framework being tested. IDF has not withdrawn from pilot zones. LAF refuses deployment until IDF withdraws. Hezbollah threatening civil war over disarmament. US sending military delegation to Lebanon. The structural incentive misalignment persists: Netanyahu needs conflict continuation for political survival. Score: L5 × I5 = 25.

riskFactors.name: Cushing Operational Floor — AT FLOOR (~20M bbl)
riskFactors.rationale: Cushing inventories remain at ~20M barrels — the operational floor. EIA fair value calculation: WTI at $101 vs ~$78 market price — $23 undervalued from fundamentals. Market pricing headline narratives; physical market pricing structural scarcity. Commodity Context confirms: ‘too much crude, not enough refined products.’ Score: L5 × I4 = 20.

riskFactors.name: 82nd Airborne Kharg Island Pre-Positioning
riskFactors.rationale: Planning intent confirmed but not activated. Activation trigger: mass-casualty event on US assets. No US casualties in current exchange cycle — calibrated exchange grammar holding. Score: L3 × I5 = 15.

riskFactors.name: US Bond / Sovereign Yield Crisis
riskFactors.rationale: UPDATED A20: 30Y at 5.07% on Sunday open — above bond vigilante threshold. 10Y at 4.59%. Oil +3.25% gap up. Long-end selling (not rallying) = market pricing stagflation, not flight-to-safety. Japan-Treasury feedback loop active. Carry trade at most extreme since 2007 (BIS: up to $11.3T). BBG: ‘Yen fix starts with pension cash coming home.’ FT: ‘Prepare for a perilous summer in markets.’ $9.7T US debt maturing 2026. Score: L5 × I5 = 25.

riskFactors.name: Stagflation Trap — Central Bank Paralysis
riskFactors.rationale: UPDATED A21: June CPI -0.4% MoM, 3.5% YoY — ceasefire mirage. Core 2.6%. Warsh: 'mission accomplished is not my view.' Fed committee split ~50/50 on hikes. July data will reverse with Brent at $85+. Crack spreads at $61/bbl (Kemp, near-record). De Haan: $4 gas in 7-10 days, $5 diesel by Friday. Bank earnings record but Dimon warns 'as good as it gets.' Score: L5 × I5 = 25.

riskFactors.name: Summer EU Gas Storage Crisis
riskFactors.rationale: UPDATED A21: EU storage 51.84% heading into H2 — below 2017-2021 average. Netherlands 29.86%, Germany 43.94%. Must reach 90% by October. Ras Laffan LNG at 45% capacity (6 of 14 trains). Zero LNG carriers through Hormuz since Jul 11. RasGas Trains 4/6 multi-year write-offs. US LNG only ramp-capable alternative but Bab el-Mandeb threat complicates Red Sea routing. Score: L5 × I5 = 25.

riskFactors.name: US Munitions Depletion — Acute
riskFactors.rationale: UPDATED A21: CNN/CSIS Jul 12: half THAAD interceptors, ~50% Patriots, ~30% Tomahawks expended. Zero THAAD deliveries in 2026. Replenishment: ~15 Tomahawk + 20 Patriot/month. Congress appropriated zero replacement funding. Now firing HIMARS/ATACMS from Bahrain into Iran — expending additional stockpiles. 3+ years to pre-war levels. Indo-Pacific deterrence gap widening. Score: L5 × I5 = 25.

riskFactors.name: Iran Oil Storage / Well Shut-in Risk — JPMorgan Physical Floor
riskFactors.rationale: JPMorgan (Jun 7): total visible inventories reach operational stress level (7.6B bbl) by June 2026 and operational floor (6.8B bbl) by September 2026. Commodity Context confirms crude oversupply but refined products structural shortage. Score: L5 × I4 = 20.

riskFactors.name: GOP Senate Hawks Blocking Deal — FULLY ACTIVE
riskFactors.rationale: Sen. Graham died during this cycle — removes one hawkish voice but institutional opposition persists. Multiple senators demanding INARA vote on any final deal. US-Iran negotiations to resume but gaps unbridgeable (ISW). Score: L5 × I4 = 20.

riskFactors.name: Agricultural Yield Failures 2026-27
riskFactors.rationale: Planting reductions locked in — harvest impact deferred to autumn 2026 and 2027. Don-Azov Canal halt affects Russian grain export logistics alongside oil products. Fertiliser shortage + diesel costs = deferred shock persisting beyond any ceasefire. Score: L4 × I4 = 16.

riskFactors.name: Ukraine Strategic Erosion
riskFactors.rationale: MIXED A20: Ukraine’s shadow fleet campaign achieved breakthrough success (~90 vessels in one week) and Russian territorial advances slowed >50% in H1 2026. But Patriot interceptor stocks exhausted. Russia oil windfall elevated. OFAC Iran revocation paradoxically supports Russian export prices. Attention still concentrated on Gulf. Score: L4 × I4 = 16.

riskFactors.name: Iran Domestic Collapse Risk
riskFactors.rationale: UPDATED A20: Mojtaba missed all 6 funeral ceremonies. NYT: barred over assassination fears. Reuters: disfigured face, wounded limbs. Jul 11: pledged revenge, stating it does not depend on his survival. No image/video/voice ever issued. Jul 14 memorial — next signal. Iran at 88.6% inflation, food +130%. OFAC revocation accelerating fiscal deterioration. Score: L4 × I5 = 20.

riskFactors.name: European Rearmament Fiscal Squeeze (NATO 3.0)
riskFactors.rationale: NATO 5% GDP benchmark. Germany ~1.9% must ramp to 3.7%. UK locked at 2.7%. Rheinmetall/MBDA developing laser weapon for German Navy (Defense News). Fourth simultaneous fiscal pressure on sovereign yields. Score: L4 × I4 = 16.

riskFactors.name: Russia-Iran $25B Rosatom Nuclear MOU — Sirik Reactors
riskFactors.rationale: $25B MOU for four Gen III reactors at Sirik. Pushes Iran toward 8,000MW. Strategic hedging continues alongside combat operations. Score: L4 × I4 = 16.

riskFactors.name: Iran Nuclear Doctrine — Pickaxe Mountain
riskFactors.rationale: UPDATED A21: Trump publicly contemplating strikes on Pickaxe Mountain — deepest underground nuclear facility near Natanz, assessed deeper than Fordow. Iran reportedly moving enriched uranium to hardened sites. IAEA access flatly refused (Baghaei: 'No'). France conditioning sanctions on full nuclear rollback. Either US has capabilities beyond public assessment or this is coercive signaling. Score: L4 × I5 = 20.

riskFactors.name: SPR Depletion — Legal Floor in Sight
riskFactors.rationale: UPDATED A21: SPR at 316.5mb (De Haan/GasBuddy). Three thresholds: 714mb authorised, 252.4mb EPCA legal floor, ~150mb structural minimum. Usable buffer above legal floor: ~64mb. At 6mb/wk draw: ~10 weeks to 252.4mb (September-October). Below legal floor, only presidential emergency finding authorises further draws — different political cost. Refinery utilisation at 96.6%, crack spreads at $61/bbl. Converges with Iran FX window and midterm pressure. Score: L5 × I5 = 25.

riskFactors.name: Hormuz Sovereignty — PGSA as Closure Weapon
riskFactors.rationale: UPDATED A21: Iran Parliament — 180 MPs demanding permanent codification of complete Hormuz control. MOU formally dead both sides. Trump claims US is 'GUARDIAN OF THE HORMUZ STRAIT.' Iran firing on ships using Omani route. Sovereignty contest now institutionally locked in on both sides. Iran deputy FM: new Omani route is proximate trigger for escalation. Score: L5 × I5 = 25.

riskFactors.name: Russia Diesel Ban + Shadow Fleet Destruction
riskFactors.rationale: UPGRADED A20 (I4→I5): Ukraine destroyed ~90 shadow fleet vessels in one week at ‘industrial scale.’ Don-Azov Canal shipping halted. Kerch Strait transit frozen. Syzran refinery (Samara) struck Jul 12. Ilsky, Omsk, Ust-Luga all hit. Russian refining one-third offline. Gazprom shares at 17-year low. Russia importing gasoline from India. Diesel crack at $72.90/bbl (series high, 3.6× norm). Ole Hansen COT: gas oil spec longs +20%. Commodity Context: US gasoline at lowest seasonal since 2014, diesel at 30-year seasonal low. Score: L5 × I5 = 25 (upgraded from 20).

riskFactors.name: Gulf State Fracture — Co-Belligerency
riskFactors.rationale: UPDATED A21: UAE tankers struck by Iranian cruise missiles in Omani waters (1 killed, 8 wounded). UAE 'reserves right to respond.' Kuwait intercepted 39 projectiles (1 ballistic, 5 cruise, 33 drones), naval vessel hit. Bahrain as ATACMS launch platform into Iran. Jordan intercepted 4 missiles. EASA warning airlines against all Gulf state airspace. Gulf states transitioning from bystanders to co-belligerents. India summoned Iran official after crew member killed. Score: L5 × I4 = 20.

riskFactors.name: Dual Chokepoint Closure (Hormuz + Bab el-Mandeb)
riskFactors.rationale: NEW A21: Houthi declared full air and naval blockade. Officials explicitly threatening Bab el-Mandeb closure alongside Hormuz. Saudi East-West Pipeline bypass to Yanbu neutralised if both close. ~25% of global oil/gas supply blocked. No modern precedent. FP, AJ, Eurasia Review all flagging. Saudi airports struck (Abha). Storm Shadow cruise missiles used on Sanaa airport. Currently declared threat, not yet fully operational. Score: L3 × I5 = 15.

riskFactors.name: Qatar LNG Disruption — Ras Laffan at 45%
riskFactors.rationale: NEW A21: Satellite thermal imagery: only 6 of 14 trains online at Ras Laffan (34.7 mtpa vs 77 mtpa nameplate). RasGas Trains 4 and 6 confirmed multi-year write-offs from March strikes. Zero LNG carriers through Hormuz since Jul 11. Direct threat to EU 90% storage mandate. EU stockpiled 140 Yamal LNG cargoes (97% to EU ports) Jan-Jun but faces 2027 ban. Score: L4 × I4 = 16.

conclusions.text: MOU formally dead — war restarted on multiple fronts. Both sides confirm: Trump called it 'only a test,' Iran says it has 'no obligations.' War Powers notification filed. Blockade reimposed. Houthi declared full Bab el-Mandeb blockade. Iran Parliament moving to codify permanent Hormuz control. China directly challenging the blockade: tanker Rich Starry transited Hormuz with ~2M bbl Iranian oil. Chinese FM blamed the US for the crisis. Only 5 vessels transited in 24 hours — all Chinese and Iranian. The analytical question is no longer whether escalation continues but whether the US wins the Battle of Hormuz before the mitigants run out — and whether China will let it.

Markets in temporary 'geopolitical anaesthesia' (Every/Rabobank). SPR draws, low Chinese imports, demand destruction, and escort workarounds are containing prices — but the window is finite. SPR usable buffer at ~64mb above the 252.4mb legal floor gives roughly 10 weeks at current draw rates. Iran's FX revenue window extends 60-90 days from blockade reimposition. Both timelines converge in September-October alongside midterm pressure and the EU storage mandate.

Dual chokepoint threat has no modern precedent. Houthi officials explicitly threatening Bab el-Mandeb closure alongside Hormuz. Saudi East-West Pipeline bypass to Yanbu — the workaround for Hormuz — is neutralised if both straits close. Ras Laffan LNG running at 45% capacity with zero LNG carriers through Hormuz since Jul 11. EU gas storage behind schedule at 51.84%.

First US strike on oil infrastructure at Kharg Island — western jetty pumping station. Previous strikes deliberately spared oil targets. This crosses a redline and validates the infrastructure half-life thesis. HIMARS/ATACMS fired from Bahrain into Iran. Kish Island water/power struck. Target set expanding from military to economic and civilian infrastructure.

'War of the refineries' creating dual supply shock. Kemp: 3-2-1 crack spread at $61/bbl, near-record (only June 2022 higher). Refined product futures at ~$130/bbl vs pre-war $80-90. Ukraine hit all 11 of Russia's largest gasoline-producing refineries. Russia diesel ban confirmed. De Haan: $4 gas in 7-10 days, $5 diesel by Friday. Two of the world's largest energy suppliers simultaneously impaired.

June CPI is a ceasefire mirage. Headline -0.4% MoM, 3.5% YoY — biggest monthly drop since April 2020. But data reflects the ceasefire window when Brent hit $68. July data collection period already pricing $85+ crude and renewed blockade. Warsh: 'mission accomplished is not my view.' Fed split ~50/50 on hikes. September hike probability at 63%.

Bank earnings underscore the structural divergence: JPM equities trading +86%, Goldman $20.98 EPS — war volatility printing record profits for trading desks. But Dimon's warning — 'it's getting close to as good as it gets' — signals that even the beneficiaries see the ceiling. US-Iraq oil MoU and Turkey pipeline route signal long-term Hormuz bypass strategy, but no near-term palliative.

changeLog.factor: Chinese Tanker Rich Starry Transits Hormuz — First Direct Blockade Defiance. Chinese FM Blames US.
changeLog.detail: Rich Starry carried ~2M bbl Iranian oil through Hormuz defying US sanctions. Chinese FM: 'The Strait was open before the war. The root cause is your illegal operations against Iran.' Only 5 vessels transited in 24h — all Chinese and Iranian, using Iran's northern lane. China testing blockade enforcement directly.

changeLog.factor: ISW: Iran Used Cluster Munitions Against Bahrain (First Time). IRGC Deploying 3,000 to Iraq Border.
changeLog.detail: Cluster munition warhead against Bahrain — previously only used against Israel. Significant tactical escalation maximising area damage. IRGC deployed 3,000 special forces (Aerospace + Ground Forces) plus drones/artillery/tanks to Iran-Iraq border targeting Kurdish opposition. US confirmed backing Saudi Sanaa airport strikes. Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf absent from first open session.

changeLog.factor: Assessment 21 — MOU Dead, War Restarted, Dual Chokepoint. Sc4 ▲ 2pp to 33% (New Briefing High).
changeLog.detail: MOU formally dead both sides. Blockade reimposed. War Powers filed. Houthi full Bab el-Mandeb blockade. First US strike on oil infrastructure (Kharg). HIMARS/ATACMS from Bahrain. Iran Parliament codifying Hormuz control. CPI ceasefire mirage (-0.4% MoM). September-October convergence: SPR legal floor + Iran FX crunch + midterms.

changeLog.factor: NEW F29: Dual Chokepoint Closure (Hormuz + Bab el-Mandeb) — L3×I5=15
changeLog.detail: Houthi declared full air/naval blockade. Saudi airports struck. Bab el-Mandeb closure explicitly threatened. Saudi Yanbu bypass neutralised if both close. ~25% global supply at risk. No modern precedent.

changeLog.factor: NEW F30: Qatar LNG Disruption — Ras Laffan at 45% Capacity — L4×I4=16
changeLog.detail: Satellite thermal: 6 of 14 trains online (34.7 mtpa vs 77 mtpa). Trains 4/6 multi-year write-offs. Zero LNG through Hormuz since Jul 11. Direct threat to EU 90% storage mandate.

changeLog.factor: CPI Ceasefire Mirage: -0.4% MoM, 3.5% YoY. Core 2.6%. Warsh: 'Not Mission Accomplished.'
changeLog.detail: June data reflects ceasefire window. Energy -5.7%. Biggest monthly drop since Apr 2020. BUT July data will reverse with Brent at $85+ and blockade. Fed split 50/50 on hikes. September hike probability 63%. De Haan: $4 gas / $5 diesel imminent.

changeLog.factor: First US Strike on Oil Infrastructure — Kharg Western Jetty. Kish Water/Power Hit.
changeLog.detail: Crosses redline Trump himself had drawn. Infrastructure half-life thesis activated. Kish Island civilian infrastructure struck (40,000 residents). Target set expanding from military to economic/civilian. HIMARS/ATACMS from Bahrain = surface-to-surface from host nation into Iran.

changeLog.factor: SPR at 316.5mb — ~64mb Usable Above 252.4mb Legal Floor. ~10 Weeks to EPCA Threshold.
changeLog.detail: Three thresholds: 714mb authorised, 252.4mb legal floor, ~150mb structural minimum. Usable buffer depleting on same timeline as Iran FX window and midterm pressure. Refinery utilisation at 96.6%, crack spreads $61/bbl.

changeLog.factor: Bank Earnings Record — JPM Equities +86%, Goldman $20.98 EPS. Dimon: 'As Good As It Gets.'
changeLog.detail: War volatility printing record bank profits. JPM $16.9B Q2 profit. Every line of business at record revenue. But Dimon warning signals peak. JPM CFO: 'complicated situation in energy and oil, particularly Hormuz.'

changeLog.factor: F28 Upgrade: Gulf States Now Co-Belligerents. UAE Tankers Struck. Kuwait 39 Projectiles Intercepted.
changeLog.detail: UAE tankers struck by Iranian cruise missiles in Omani waters (1 killed, 8 wounded). Kuwait intercepted 1 ballistic + 5 cruise missiles + 33 drones, naval vessel hit. Bahrain as ATACMS launch platform. Jordan intercepted 4 missiles. EASA warning on all Gulf state airspace.

changeLog.factor: Assessment 20 — IRGC Declares Hormuz Closed. Sc4 ▲ 5pp to 31% (Briefing High). Institutional Lock-In Active.
changeLog.detail: Three converging institutional signals define A20: IRGC formally declared Hormuz closed (operational), Iran parliament advancing 14-article Hormuz Management Bill (legislative), Mojtaba committed to closure leverage in first statement as supreme leader (doctrinal). These are not reversible. ISW: 300+ target strikes had ‘no visible effect’ on Iran’s calculus. Iran retaliated against 6 countries including mediator states. Sc4 at 31% (highest ever). Sc2 at 4% (lowest ever). F19 upgraded L3→L4 (nuclear doctrine). F27 upgraded I4→I5 (shadow fleet destruction). F28 added (Gulf State Fracture).

changeLog.factor: Diesel Crack Spread Record $72.90/bbl — Dual Supply Shock Intensifying
changeLog.detail: U.S. diesel crack spread hit series high at $72.90/bbl (3.6× seasonal norm). Russia diesel ban confirmed. Ukraine destroyed ~90 shadow fleet vessels in one week, halted Don-Azov Canal. Russian refining one-third offline. Syzran/Ilsky/Omsk refineries struck. Gasoline at $125/bbl equivalent, diesel at $140/bbl. Commodity Context: ‘too much crude, not enough refined products.’ Gas oil spec longs +20%. F27 upgraded to L5×I5=25.

changeLog.factor: Gulf State Fracture — Mediator States Struck, No Brokers Remain
changeLog.detail: Iran struck all mediator states including Oman’s Duqm Port 24h after hosting Araghchi. WSJ: Paragraph 5 ambiguity was deliberate. UAE/Bahrain/Kuwait vs Oman/Qatar split on response. Sen. Graham and former Qatari Emir died. F28 added (L4×I4=16, High). Sc2 lowest ever at 4%.

changeLog.factor: F19 Nuclear Doctrine Upgrade — Parchin Hardening + Parliamentarian Rhetoric
changeLog.detail: F19 upgraded L3→L4 (score 15→20). Multiple parliamentarians discussing doctrine change. CNN/IISS: Parchin Taleghan 2 being hardened with concrete roof. Physical indicators plus rhetorical escalation justify upgrade.

changeLog.factor: Assessment 19 — Active Combat Day 3. Dual Supply Shock (Iran + Russia). SPR Chart Added. Sc2 ▼3pp to 7%. Sc4 ▲3pp to 26%.
changeLog.detail: Three consecutive nights of US strikes totalling 170+ targets. Iran retaliating across 4 countries. Russia banned diesel exports Jul 8 after 42.7% of refining disabled. Euro diesel margin record $60.17/bbl. SPR at 319.5mb drawing 6.2mb/wk. F20 upgraded. F27 added. SPR chart added.

changeLog.factor: Assessment 18 — MOU Declared Dead. Trump: ‘It’s Over.’ Sc2 collapses -12pp to 10%. Sc3 dominant at 65%. Sc4 +7pp to 23%.
changeLog.detail: Trump declared MOU ‘over’ at NATO summit. US struck 80+ targets. Iran retaliated against 85 sites. OFAC revoked oil export license. Southern corridor eliminated.

changeLog.factor: 30-Year Treasury Yield Breaches 5%. Financial Stress Threshold Crossed.
changeLog.detail: 30Y at 5.068%. 10Y at 4.578%. Japan spent ¥11.73T defending yen, fully retraced. Carry trade at most negative since 2007.

changeLog.factor: 60 IRGC Gunboats Destroyed + Strait Arsenal Degraded
changeLog.detail: CENTCOM targeted fast attack craft fleet plus air defenses, coastal radar, anti-ship missile sites across southern Iran.

changeLog.factor: IRGC-Government Split Now Public. Mojtaba Still Invisible.
changeLog.detail: Police arrested hardline IRGC supporters at funeral. Government using legal MOU language; IRGC launching missiles at Gulf states.

changeLog.factor: Assessment 17 — Sc4 ▲6pp. Sc3→Sc4 Reframed as Continuum. Market Mispricing at Historic Extremes.
changeLog.detail: Sc1 8→5%. Sc2 38→22%. Sc3 45→57%. Sc4 9→16%. Deepest intelligence collection in series history.

changeLog.factor: Khamenei Funeral (Jul 4-9): Succession Crisis Deepens — Mojtaba Absent
changeLog.detail: Six-day funeral. Mojtaba absent all days — physically incapacitated. Reuters: facial disfigurement.

changeLog.factor: Hormuz Governance: Iran Declares Fees ‘Definite’ — Two Competing Architectures
changeLog.detail: PGSA operating. 40+ IRGC speedboats on satellite imagery. France/UK deploy minehunters. OFAC exposure at engagement.

changeLog.factor: Market Mispricing at Historic Extremes
changeLog.detail: Highest Brent short positioning vs lowest global onshore inventories in recorded history.

changeLog.factor: Assessment 16 — Sc3 Overtakes Sc2. MOU Collapse Conditions Accumulating.
changeLog.detail: Sc3 32→45%. Sc2 47→38%. Rabobank 50% re-escalation base case.

changeLog.factor: Assessment 15 — MOU First 72 Hours: Stress-Testing the Framework
changeLog.detail: PGSA live. Lebanon ceasefire fraud. DPA Section 708 signed. Shipping costs locking Jul 1.
changeLog.scope: Overview · Risk Register · Energy · Food · Sources

changeLog.factor: Assessment 14 — MOU Signed: Sc2 Now Base Case.
changeLog.detail: MOU signed Jun 17. Sc2 37→47%. Oil ~20% drop. Fed hawkish.

changeLog.factor: Assessment 13 — MOU Agreed: Largest Scenario Shift
changeLog.detail: Sc2 +20pp. Sc3 -25pp. Fars: toll-free 60 days only.

changeLog.factor: Assessment 12 — Direct US-Iran Kinetic Exchange + Sc3 to 68%
changeLog.detail: Sc3 62→68% (+6pp). ISW: Iran believes it is at war.

changeLog.factor: Assessments 1–11 Archived (May 13 – Jun 9)
changeLog.detail: 22 entries covering full history. Full archive available on request.

maritime.notes: Hormuz transits at 5-week low (14 on Jul 12 per Kpler). 52% WoW decline. Omani corridor abandoned. Jul 14: only 5 merchant vessels transited in 24h — all Chinese and Iranian — using Iran's northern lane. Chinese tanker Rich Starry carried ~2M bbl Iranian oil through Hormuz defying US sanctions. Zero LNG carriers through Hormuz since Jul 11. LVision: minimum HYA exports at 79% of pre-war level via dark/escorted routes. Blockade reimposed Jul 14 20:00 GMT. JMIC raised threat level to 'severe' — 4 tanker attacks logged Jul 13.
